metadata.summary: Design agent memory: short-term conversation context, long-term vector store, episodic task memory, context overflow prevention via summarization and relevance filtering.

content.title: Agent Memory System — Short-Term, Long-Term, and Context Management
content.summary: Autonomous agents running long tasks overflow their context window without explicit memory management. A three-tier memory architecture — short-term conversation buffer, long-term vector store, episodic task log — keeps agents grounded across sessions. Summarize conversation history every N steps; use cosine similarity recall to inject only relevant past experiences rather than raw history.

why.p: Context overflow is one of the three most common autonomous agent failure modes (alongside infinite loops and tool hallucination). Without memory management, a long-running agent either hits the context limit and fails silently, or loops because it forgot it already tried an approach. A structured memory system converts experience into recall-able facts, enabling cross-session learning and preventing repeated mistakes.

The three-tier split mirrors human working memory: short-term holds the active task context (last 10-20 exchanges), long-term persists compressed knowledge in a vector store (episodes, facts, tools learned), episodic records task outcomes for future planning. Each tier has a distinct eviction policy: short-term evicts by count; long-term never evicts; episodic evicts by age or relevance score.

list.item: Long-running agents that execute more than 20 steps in a single session.
Agents that handle recurring similar tasks and should learn from past runs.
Agents that must remember user preferences or domain knowledge across sessions.
Multi-session research or content generation workflows where context exceeds 50K tokens.
Single-turn agents completing in under 10 steps — memory overhead exceeds value.
Tasks where all necessary context fits in one context window — adding memory layers introduces complexity with no benefit.
Strict reproducibility requirements — memory recall is non-deterministic, which conflicts with auditing needs.

section.name: memory-tiers
section.p: The agent memory system has three tiers with different storage backends, eviction policies, and access patterns:

list.item: Short-term: conversation buffer, last N exchanges, in-memory list. Evicted FIFO when count exceeds max. Always included in context verbatim.

Long-term: vector store (Qdrant, Chroma, pgvector). Persists compressed summaries and facts. Recalled by cosine similarity against the current query. Include only top-k results above relevance threshold.
Episodic: task outcome log. Records "Task X → Result Y" entries. Used by planning agents to avoid strategies that previously failed.

rule.statement: Agents MUST summarize conversation history every N steps (N = 10-20) when running loops longer than the summarization threshold. The summary MUST preserve all task-relevant facts, active tool states, and key decisions; it MUST drop redundant exchanges and intermediate reasoning steps.
rule.rationale: Without summarization, context grows linearly with steps. At 20 steps of ReAct with verbose tool outputs, most 128K-token models hit their limit. Summarization compresses 80% of history into 10% of tokens while preserving the facts needed for the next steps.

rule.statement: Long-term memory recall MUST use a relevance threshold (cosine similarity >= 0.7 by default). The agent MUST NOT inject all stored memories into the context — only the top-k (k=3-5) memories above threshold. The recall query MUST be the current task or goal text, not a generic "what do I know" query.
rule.rationale: Injecting all memories into every prompt defeats the purpose of the memory system and can overflow the context window. Thresholded top-k recall injects only the facts that are semantically relevant to the current task, keeping the context lean.

example.title: Hybrid memory implementation
example.code:
from datetime import datetime import numpy as np from typing import List, Dict, Callable, Optional
class Memory: """Agent memory with short-term and long-term storage."""
def __init__( self, embedding_func: Callable, max_short_term: int = 10, relevance_threshold: float = 0.7 ): self.embedding_func = embedding_func self.max_short_term = max_short_term self.relevance_threshold = relevance_threshold self.short_term: List[Dict] = [] self.long_term: List[Dict] = [] # Use vector DB in production
def add(self, content: str, metadata: Dict = None): """Add memory entry.""" entry = { "content": content, "timestamp": datetime.now().isoformat(), "metadata": metadata or {}, "embedding": self.embedding_func(content) } self.short_term.append(entry) if len(self.short_term) > self.max_short_term: old_entry = self.short_term.pop(0) self.long_term.append(old_entry)
def recall(self, query: str, k: int = 5) -> List[Dict]: """Recall relevant memories by cosine similarity.""" query_embedding = self.embedding_func(query) all_memories = self.short_term + self.long_term if not all_memories: return [] similarities = [] for memory in all_memories: sim = np.dot(query_embedding, memory["embedding"]) / ( np.linalg.norm(query_embedding) * np.linalg.norm(memory["embedding"]) ) similarities.append((memory, sim)) relevant = [ (m, s) for m, s in similarities if s >= self.relevance_threshold ] relevant.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True) return [m for m, _ in relevant[:k]]
def summarize_recent(self) -> str: """Summarize recent memories for context injection.""" if not self.short_term: return "No recent memories." recent = self.short_term[-5:] return "\n".join([m["content"] for m in recent])
example.note: In production, replace self.long_term list with Qdrant or pgvector for scalable cosine search. The in-memory version is suitable for development and short sessions.

antipattern.description: Storing all conversation history in the system prompt without summarization or relevance filtering.
antipattern.reason: History grows linearly with iterations. At 30+ steps the system prompt alone exceeds most model context limits. The agent then silently truncates or fails. Summarize every 10-20 steps and recall only what is relevant.

antipattern.description: Using CrewAI agent "memory" without configuring a persistent vector store across runs.
antipattern.reason: CrewAI agent memory is not persistent across runs by default. Without a configured vector store backend, the agent loses all learned context when the process restarts.

section.name: context-management-prompts
section.p: Two prompt patterns are required for memory-augmented agents: a summarization prompt called every N steps to compress history, and a memory recall prompt to format retrieved memories for injection into the next step.

checklist.check: Short-term memory capped at 10-20 entries with FIFO eviction to long-term.
Long-term storage uses a real vector DB in production (Qdrant, pgvector, Chroma).
Recall uses cosine similarity with threshold >= 0.7 and top-k = 3-5.
Summarization triggered every N steps (N configured per agent type).
Episodic memory records task outcome ("Task: X → Result: Y") after each run.
Memory store is initialized once per agent and reused across sessions.
